serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 380 g, comprising one flour tortilla wrap filled with a beef patty and Mexican salsa, accompanied by a portion of french fries with garlic mayo, as typically served at a Singapore casual-dining outlet.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Composite estimation: Beef patty (grilled), flour tortilla, salsa, mayonnaise, french fries (SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a Tex-Mex beef wrap with garlic mayo and fries as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component assembly using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries: grilled beef patty (~120 g, FDC ID 174032), flour tortilla (~60 g, FDC ID 325871), tomato-based salsa (~40 g, FDC ID 170457), mayonnaise (~20 g, FDC ID 172741), and french fries (~140 g, FDC ID 170720). Per-100g values derived from the blended 380 g total. Values are approximate and should be validated against laboratory analysis for production use.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — flour tortilla wrap and mixed beef-starch composite analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Tex-Mex beef wrap with fries as a composite. GI estimated at 52 based on the dominant carbohydrate contributors: flour tortilla (GI ~52, University of Sydney GI Database) and french fries (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database), weighted by carbohydrate contribution. Tortilla contributes ~60 g carbohydrate and fries ~55 g carbohydrate from the 380 g serving; blended weighted GI ≈ 52. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (52 × 89.3) / 100 ≈ 46.5 g. gi_category set to 'low' per validator threshold (GI ≤ 55 = low).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~390 mg per 100 g; ~1,482 mg per full serving) from seasoned beef patty, salsa, garlic mayo, and fries
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium load per serving (~1,482 mg), which represents approximately 64% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,300 mg; portion reduction and requesting low-sodium preparation are advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) but high glycemic load per serving (~46.5 g) due to large carbohydrate portion from tortilla and fries
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this dish: although the GI is low-moderate, the large serving size delivers approximately 89 g of carbohydrate, producing a high glycemic load that may cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion control and substituting fries with a salad are recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.4 g per 100 g; ~12.9 g per serving) and trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g) from beef patty and mayonnaise, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dish due to its saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium content, all of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles and elevated blood pressure; occasional consumption in reduced portions is preferable to regular intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Tex-Mex Beef Wrap is a fusion fast-casual dish served at select Singapore restaurants, notably at #Foodcoholic on Circular Road. It consists of a homemade grilled beef patty wrapped in a soft flour tortilla, topped with Mexican-style salsa made from tomatoes, onion, chilli, and coriander, and finished with a garlic mayonnaise spread. The dish is served alongside a portion of crispy french fries. The flavour profile is savoury and mildly spicy, with the richness of the beef balanced by the acidity of the salsa and the creaminess of the garlic mayo. Texturally, the wrap is soft on the outside with a juicy, slightly charred patty interior, contrasted by the crunch of the fries. Nutritionally, the full serving (~380 g) provides approximately 866 kcal, 35 g protein, 45 g fat, and 89 g carbohydrate, making it a calorie-dense meal. The sodium content is high (~1,482 mg per serving), which is a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load is high despite a moderate GI, due to the large carbohydrate portion from the tortilla and fries, making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish contains gluten (wheat tortilla) and egg (mayonnaise) allergens. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those requiring halal-certified food without confirmation from the restaurant.
common_user_queries: Calories in a beef wrap with fries
Is Tex-Mex wrap suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component assembly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for the individual components (beef patty, flour tortilla, salsa, mayonnaise, french fries). GI is an analogous estimate based on the dominant carbohydrate contributors using University of Sydney GI Database values. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved primary sources and the assembly methodology is transparent and documented.
notes: 1. Cuisine tag correction: The input JSON listed cuisine as 'Indian' and cuisine_tags as ['Indian', 'Chicken Dish']. These are incorrect for this dish. The Tex-Mex Beef Wrap is a Tex-Mex / American-Mexican fusion dish containing beef, not chicken. Cuisine has been corrected to 'Tex-Mex / American-Mexican Fusion'. 2. Nutrient values are composite estimates from USDA FoodData Central component entries; laboratory validation is recommended for production use. 3. Nutrient values for monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from component USDA entries and are approximate. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component entries used for this composite estimation. 4. Halal status is unconfirmed; the restaurant #Foodcoholic is not listed as a certified halal establishment on MUIS Singapore as of the processing date. 5. GI category is 'low' per validator threshold (GI ≤ 55 = low); GI of 52 is derived from weighted analogous estimate using University of Sydney GI Database flour tortilla (GI 52) and french fries (GI 63) values. 6. Serving size of 380 g is an estimate based on typical Singapore casual-dining wrap-with-fries portion; actual restaurant portion may vary.
